---
title: "![](images/icons/icon_11.png){.title-icon} Add variables to your cohort"
subtitle: "Extract outcomes and covariates — filtered to exactly your cohort"
date: last-modified
---

You have built your cohort ([Phase 10](10_byg-din-kohorte.qmd)) — a table with `pnr` and `index_date` per person. Now you **extract** the variables you need: outcomes and covariates. On this page you pull out each variable as its own table; in [Phase 12](11_data-manipulation.qmd) you assemble them into one analysis dataset.

::: {.callout-important}
**Always filter to your cohort — before `collect()`.**
You only need data on the people you have built. So filter each extraction to your cohort **while it is still lazy** (in Arrow/DuckDB), so you do not pull the entire population into R:

```r
kohort      <- readRDS("datasets/full_cohort.rds")
cohort_pnrs <- unique(kohort$pnr)          # vector with ALL pnr (exposed + comparator cohort)

register <- open_dataset("path/to/register/") %>%
  rename_with(tolower) %>%
  filter(pnr %in% !!cohort_pnrs) %>%        # !! sends your R vector to DuckDB — see Phase 5
  select(pnr, ...) %>%                       # select only the columns you need
  collect()                                  # ONLY now is data pulled into R
```

Alternatively `semi_join()` against your cohort table. `%in% !!cohort_pnrs` is simplest when you just have a pnr vector. Filter and `select()` **before** `collect()` — the single most important rule for speed and memory (see [Phase 5](05_universelle-moenster.qmd)).
:::

---

## Which variables?

| Type | Page | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **Outcomes** (event date, censoring) | [Outcomes](11a_udfald.qmd) | LPR (diagnoses), DODSAARS (death), VNDS (emigration) |
| **Socioeconomics** (education, income, employment) | [Socioeconomic variables](13_socioekonomiske-variable.qmd) | UDDA, FAIK, AKM |
| **Comorbidity** (Charlson, multimorbidity) | [Comorbidity](11c_komorbiditet.qmd) | LPR + ready-made algorithms (NMI/`comorbidity`) |
| **Demographics** (age, sex) | covered in [Phase 6](06_foerste-udtraek.qmd) | BEF |

Each page shows the same pattern: open the register → filter to the cohort → select/derive the variable → `collect()` → save as `.rds`. Once all variables are extracted, you assemble them in [Phase 12 — Assemble and prepare the dataset](11_data-manipulation.qmd).

---

## See also

- [Phase 10 — Build your study population](10_byg-din-kohorte.qmd) — the cohort you filter to
- [Phase 12 — Assemble and prepare the dataset](11_data-manipulation.qmd) — join all extracts into one dataset
- [Algorithms & special packages](14a_pakker-oversigt.qmd) — ready-made tools (OSDC, NMI) to derive variables
